gps.name: Global Preferences Survey (GPS)
gps.description: Globally representative dataset on economic preferences. Experimentally validated survey module measuring 6 preference dimensions for 80,000 individuals across 76 countries (Gallup World Poll 2012). Covers 90% of world population and global income.
gps.methodology: Experimentally validated survey module (quantitative staircase + qualitative self-assessment items per dimension)

papers.t: Global Evidence on Economic Preferences
papers.authors: Falk, Becker, Dohmen, Enke, Huffman, Sunde

papers.t: Relationship of gender differences in preferences to economic development and gender equality
The preference survey module: A validated instrument for measuring risk, time, and social preferences
Patience and Comparative Development
Ancient origins of the global variation in economic preferences

soep.name: German Socio-Economic Panel (SOEP)
soep.description: Longest-running representative longitudinal household panel in Germany (since 1984). Includes experimentally validated risk and trust preference measures used extensively in behavioral economics research. SOEP-IS (Innovation Sample) allows custom experimental modules.

key_papers.t: Individual Risk Attitudes: New Evidence from a Large, Representative, Experimentally-Validated Survey
key_papers.j: Journal of the European Economic Association
key_papers.authors: Dohmen, Falk, Huffman, Sunde, Schupp, Wagner

key_papers.t: A nation-wide laboratory: Examining trust and trustworthiness by integrating behavioral experiments into representative survey
key_papers.authors: Fehr, Fischbacher, von Rosenbladt, Schupp, Wagner

wvs.name: World Values Survey (WVS)
wvs.description: Global research project exploring people's values, beliefs, and their impact on social and political development. Covers ~100 countries across 7 waves since 1981. Includes generalized trust question widely used in economics.

ess.name: European Social Survey (ESS)
ess.description: Biennial cross-national survey of social attitudes and values across 30+ European countries since 2001. High methodological standards with rigorous translation protocols. Includes trust, reciprocity, and subjective wellbeing measures.

gallup_world_poll.name: Gallup World Poll
gallup_world_poll.description: Annual survey covering 160+ countries on wellbeing, economic confidence, governance, and social issues. Infrastructure used for GPS data collection. Cantril Self-Anchoring Striving Scale for life evaluation widely used in happiness research.
gallup_world_poll.note: GPS was collected within framework of Gallup World Poll 2012
